---
title: "Recap"
subtitle: "Tools, structured data, & chatbots"
format:
  revealjs:
    theme: [default, theme.scss]
    slide-number: c/t
    width: 1280
    height: 720
    incremental: false
    transition: fade
    code-line-numbers: false
---

## Where we've been

* 🔧 **Tools**: give LLMs real capabilities

::: fragment
* 📦 **Structured data extraction**: turn text/images/PDFs into typed data
:::

::: fragment
* 💬 **shinychat**: turn a chat into an app. 
    * Customize with tool displays, slash commands, and more
:::


## The big idea, again

**Agent = LLM + tools + loop** — tools are how LLMs learn and act on the world.

::: fragment
A tool is just a **typed, documented function** — the model decides when to call it.
:::

::: fragment
Letting an LLM execute arbitrary code is powerful but risky — **sandbox it, run it remotely, or restrict its scope**.
:::

::: fragment
Structured output turns LLM responses into **data your code can branch on**, not just prose.
:::

## Tools & data, so far

`register_tool()` turns a Python function into something the model can call.

::: fragment
**MCP** extends tools beyond your own process — over HTTP or STDIO.
:::

::: fragment
`.chat_structured()` + a **Pydantic model** extracts typed data from text, images, or PDFs.
:::

::: fragment
`str | None` fields handle data that's sometimes missing.
:::

## shinychat, so far {.center}

::: fragment
`chat.app()` is a thin wrapper — `shinychat` is what's actually powering it.
:::

::: fragment
`client=` gives you history, tool display, streaming, cancel, attachments, error handling — **for free**.
:::

::: fragment
Return a `ToolResultDisplay` to show the user a polished view while the model sees raw data.
:::

## Quick check 🙋🏻‍♀️

What two things does a Python function need to become a tool?

::: fragment
Name one way to keep LLM-driven code execution safe.
:::

::: fragment
What does `client=` give you for free in `shinychat`?
:::

## Up next {.center background-color="#0F3B5D"}

📊 **querychat** — the capstone: self-service analytics

::: fragment
✔️ **Measuring correctness** — evals
:::

::: fragment
🧪 **Production tooling** — observability
:::
